achievements.description: Mike published 'Future Phase Approach for Betting Tips Hub' on Confluence — a 5-phase product roadmap for the Betting Tips product. Phase 1 (Tips Hub) is Done. Phase 2 covers Pre-Defined Accas from the Matrix widget and Bet Builders with deep-linking into operator betslips. Phase 3 is a Sport Index (/betting-tips/[sport]) with date-based pagination. Phase 4 is a League Index (/betting-tips/football/[league]). Phase 5 is the full Match Page with per-market tips, confidence indicators, and archived results for SEO value. Significant forward planning document for the product.
achievements.source: Confluence — Future Phase Approach for Betting Tips Hub (Mike Pett, GDC Core, ~16:30 BST)

achievements.description: T&Cs hybrid approach decision reached in #gdc-pdmt: scrollable terms for all operators by default, static terms hardcoded for Bet365 and any future brands with compliance mandates. Mike proposed the hybrid solution; Gabriel Cornoiu added the operator grid exclusion recommendation (exclude brands with full static T&Cs from the UK grid for visual consistency); Ciara Carroll confirmed design feasibility. Translated directly into GDCU-8862 moving to In Product today.
achievements.source: Slack #gdc-pdmt + Jira GDCU-8862 (Kelvin Yee → In Product 10:51 BST)

achievements.description: Jessica Dordevic confirmed back from sick day — GDCU-8694 (Image Carousel 500x290) moved from In Progress to In Product at 12:50 BST, the new carousel size delivered ahead of expectation. Removes the uncertainty flagged in this morning's report. GDCU-8546 (Tinder Widget) and GDCU-8879 (Genesis Brand Carousel) remain In Progress.
achievements.source: Jira GDCU-8694 (Jessica Dordevic → In Product 12:50 BST)

achievements.description: Four tickets progressed to In QA today, clearing dev-complete work into the test queue: GDCU-8840 Instagram Headers Widget NZ (Novica Vukobratovic), GDCU-8842 Integrate Genesis Content Layouts (Harden Thomas Wallace), GDCU-8870 Performance Improvements for Page Timeouts (Nebojsa Stanic), GDCU-8863 Fix Incorrect Ktag CT Value on Gambling.com Offers (Sanja Budic).

F2P App strategic direction aligned in #gdc-f2p-apps: Dean Ryan proposed pivoting the app to a Cosmo-focused experience with a Slots Library and free spins/slot offers. Colin Brannigan approved. Gabriel Cornoiu proposing Claude to design the V2 POC UI, with IUJ integration deferred to a later phase. Dean noted some F2P games are broken and the Cosmo Spins daily limit was triggering — these are smoke-test items for the next iteration.

Ciara Carroll attended a Claude Code to Figma webinar and confirmed GDC's design-dev high-agency workflow is ahead of the industry: the Q&A showed attendees were asking about the exact approach GDC is already using. Validation of the high-agency strategy Mike and Gabriel have been driving.

Publishing Regression Testing and CMS Regression Testing both completed for the 24/04/2026 release — checklists run and documented in Confluence by Dean Blackborough, Claire Cordina, and Nicholas Carvalho.
achievements.source: Confluence — [Publishing Regression Testing] - 24/04/2026 and [CMS Regression Testing] - 24/04/2026

achievements.description: Instagram Offers Carousel rectangular logo blocker identified and worked through in #gdc-product-design-pod: no suitable logos existed in Origins at 258×145px. Chloe, Miguel and Ciara aligned on trialling Operators - 500px - Brand Product - XXX as the closest available source. Pending Miguel testing whether this renders acceptably at the required dimensions before a decision on new Origins assets is made.

Two additional tickets reached In Product today (in addition to those noted in the afternoon report): GDCU-8824 Apply Event Tag for Cosmo Spins/Slot Tournament Games (Kelvin Yee) and GDCU-8807 Set Up Dynamic Content API via Cloudflare Workers + D1 (Kelvin Yee). Both awaiting Mike's review Monday.

meeting_summaries.title: Mid Week Catchup — 22 April 2026 (most recent documented meeting; context reference)
meeting_summaries.summary: Wednesday mid-week catchup. Participants: Ciara Carroll, Chloe Christie, Gabriel Cornoiu, Eoin McCarthy, Shane McGrath, Shalini Kumar, Mike Pett, Joshua Smith. Gabriel raised design readiness questions for four features ahead of next Thursday's sprint planning. Chloe raised the Instagram A/B test logo issue (GDCU-8840) and the Strategy Hub Thursday release logistics. Eoin confirmed a PSI browser agent fix was being tested that day (this became GDCU-8869). Shane's Sign In/Up returning-user question was resolved by Jessica. Joshua confirmed Image Carousel dimensions were correct.
decisions: Sign In/Up Welcome Back screen for returning users confirmed resolved — spoke with Jessica
Global Strategy Hub release checklist defined: GX Casino articles finished by EOD Wednesday, dev removes localised hubs/articles, manual EN→GX redirects to be set up
PSI browser agent fix identified by Sanja — Eoin to test (resulted in GDCU-8869 moving to In Product today)
action_items_from_meeting: Confirm design readiness by next Thursday planning for: Always On Betting Tips Page, Tinder Style Offer Match widget, ACE Betslips, ACE Assistant Widget
Instagram logo approach decision needed: test existing Origins 500px assets vs. raise dedicated per-brand logo tickets (GDCU-8840)

action_points.action: URGENT MONDAY FIRST — Unblock GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign (Ciara Carroll). This is now entering Day 10 blocked and In Product over the weekend. First action Monday morning: identify root cause and clear or escalate to Gabriel or Colin. Both GDCU-8619 and GDCU-8801 are blocking Ciara's sprint output and will impact sprint velocity into the new week.

Review and sign off GDCU-8869 Desktop PSI geo-redirect race condition (Sanja Budic, High priority). In Product since 12:48 today — PSI desktop audits are broken across 78+ URLs in all markets because isGooglePageSpeedInsightsAgent() is undefined when the redirect fires. Fix options fully documented in the ticket. High-priority review for Monday.

GDCU-8792 Cloudflare Bot Management (Sanja Budic) — remains Blocked in QA across the weekend. Assess Monday morning whether this requires escalation to unblock. Sanja now has two concurrent PM items (GDCU-8869 In Product + GDCU-8792 Blocked) — address GDCU-8869 first, then assess GDCU-8792.

GDCU-8893 Gambling.com Design System (Ciara Carroll, Highest priority) — still In Progress, Netlify deployment restriction unresolved since this morning. Check in Monday on revised timeline. With GDCU-8619 also Blocked, Ciara is carrying the most risk of any team member into next week.

Review and sign off GDCU-8862 Offer Terms Static/Scrollable (Kelvin Yee). Moved to In Product at 10:51 today. Bet365 compliance requirement — per-brand static/scrollable T&C logic for all UK operator lists. T&Cs hybrid approach was agreed in #gdc-pdmt today, so this should be straightforward to sign off.

Review and sign off GDCU-8694 Image Carousel 500x290 (Jessica Dordevic). Moved to In Product at 12:50 today. Adds new 500×290 size option to the image carousel shortcode for all geos. Jessica's first delivery back after sick day.

Review and sign off GDCU-8801 CA/FR Oplist Redesign (Ciara Carroll). In Product since 16 April — will be 11 days by Monday. UX improvements to CA/FR casino page oplist (card refinements, search, filter, compare). Sign-off needed alongside GDCU-8619 to fully unblock Ciara.

Review and sign off GDCU-8512 Always On Betting Tips Page (Miguel Migneco). In Product since 23 April 12:06 — no action taken today. Mobile-first Betting Tips Hub with live tip cards, sport navigation tabs, Matrix widget, and operator offers module. Stakeholders: Colin Brannigan, Dean Ryan, Dan Smullen, Chloe Christie.

Review and sign off GDCU-8824 Apply Event Tag for Cosmo Spins / Slot Tournament Games (Kelvin Yee). Moved to In Product today. Mike listed as stakeholder — tracking implementation for Cosmo Spins game-end events.

Review and sign off GDCU-8807 Dynamic Content API via Cloudflare Workers + D1 (Kelvin Yee). Moved to In Product today. Significant infrastructure piece enabling CMS-driven dynamic content delivery without server-side changes.

Reply to Chloe Christie in Group DM (Mike / Chloe / Gab) re: stakeholder intake form — she asked to add a schema sub-section to the SEO field and a new page option with a dropdown for editable content blocks (2026-04-23 15:26 BST). No new Slack activity from Chloe today — confirm whether her feedback has been addressed.
action_points.source: Slack — Group DM (carried from morning + afternoon reports)

action_points.action: Reply to Gabriel Cornoiu in Group DM re: stakeholder intake form — two open questions still unanswered: (1) should there be an 'Other' option in the offer ordering dropdown? (2) does 'new ordering structure required' mean a new OpList? No new Slack activity from Gab on this today.

If not, decision needed: create new dedicated Origins assets via UX or raise a separate Origins ticket. GDCU-8840 is in QA but the logo issue blocks final sign-off.
